title: Delissea palakea
taxonomy: Delissea palakea is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is native to Hawaii, specifically the islands of Kauai and Oahu. Delissea palakea is a member of the Campanulaceae family, which includes other plants commonly known as bellflowers. The Campanulaceae family is further classified under the order Asterales, and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification indicates that Delissea palakea is a flowering plant with characteristics typical of its family and order.
uses: Delissea palakea, also known as Ma'o, is a plant native to Hawaii with various traditional medicinal uses. It has been used by Native Hawaiians to treat respiratory issues and coughs due to its expectorant properties. The plant is said to help relieve congestion and promote the removal of mucus from the body. Additionally, it has been used to treat colds, fever, and sore throats. Delissea palakea's medicinal properties are still being studied for their potential benefits in modern medicine.
